message_text: I want to give an update about my remaining loan payment. I will not be able to pay this month.

I’m an AI agent acting on behalf of Nira, and this conversation is being logged. I’m contacting you about your Nira loan ending ****4831. To verify I’m speaking with the right person, please confirm the last four digits of the account reference.
I already told you I can’t pay, and I’m not giving more details right now.
Understood. I’ve noted that you cannot pay this month and do not want to provide more details right now. I’m completing the assessment and passing this to the next step.

I’m out of work right now, so I’m not promising any payment.

Understood. Based on Nira’s current policy, I can’t set any extension beyond 30 days, and you’ve said you’re out of work and not promising any payment. I’ve recorded that hardship position. If your situation changes and you can review a lender-approved hardship or resolution option within the policy window, that can be checked then.

I said what I needed to say—I’m unemployed and I’m not committing to any payment.

Noted. You have confirmed that you are unemployed and are not committing to any payment. No concrete approved option is currently available on that basis. If your situation changes and you are ready to review a policy-compliant option, that can be checked then.
